3 L.R.O. 2002 Financial Administration (Public CAP. 5A
Enterprise Investment Fund) ss.1-4
CHAPTER 5A
FINANCIAL ADMINISTRATION (PUBLIC
ENTERPRISE INVESTMENT FUND)
An Act to provide for the establishment of a Fund into which revenues
1993-19.
realised from the disposal of certain public assets are to be paid, 1998-3.
2001/42.
and for related matters.
[20th December, 1993]
Commence-
ment.
1. This Act may be cited as the Financial Administration (Public
Short title.
Enterprise Investment Fund) Act.
2. (1) The purposes of this Act are
Purposes of
the Act.
(a) to establish a fund to be known as the "Public Enterprise
Investment Fund" into which revenue representing the proceeds
of the disposal of an enterprise or shares or other interests in
an enterprise owned by the Government shall be paid; and
(b) to provide for the application of the resources of that Fund to
finance
(i) the repayment of public debt;
(ii) the rehabilitation of enterprises in the public sector; and
(iii) specific investment projects.
(2) This Act shall be given such fair, large and liberal construction
and interpretation as would best ensure the attainment of its purposes.
3. For the purposes of this Act "enterprise" means an enterprise
Interpreta-
specified in the Schedule. tion.
Schedule.
4. The Public Enterprise Investment Fund, in this Act referred to
Establish-
as "the Fund", is established by this Act. ment of
Fund.
THE LAWS OF BARBADOS
Printed by the Government Printer, Bay Street, St. Michael
by the authority of the Government of Barbados

CAP. 5A Financial Administration (Public L.R.O. 2002 4
ss.5-7 Enterprise Investment Fund)
5. The purposes of the Fund are
Purposes of
Fund.
(a) to receive all revenue
(i) representing the proceeds from the disposal of an
enterprise, or derived from the sale of shares or other
interests in an enterprise, owned by the Government;
(ii) representing a return on any investment undertaken by
the Government out of the resources of the Fund;
(b) to defray the costs associated with the sale of an enterprise or
any interest in an enterprise;
(c) to finance specific investment projects;
(d) to finance the repayment of any public debt; and
(e) to defray the costs incurred in the administration of this Act.
5A. Notwithstanding the provisions of section 5, Parliament may
Transfer of
revenue by enactment authorise the transfer of revenue from the Fund for the
from Fund.
creation of any other specific investment fund.
1998-3.
6. The Fund shall be administered by the Accountant-General
Administra-
tion of Fund. under the control and direction of the Minister.
7. The resources of the Fund are
Resources
of Fund.
(a) the revenue representing the proceeds of the disposal of an
enterprise, or shares or other interests in an enterprise, owned
by the Government;
(b) such money as Parliament may provide for the purpose;
(c) all sums representing the payment of debts or the discharge of
any liability to the Government by an enterprise;
(d) all sums made available to the Fund by way of gift, grant or
otherwise.

5 L.R.O. 2002 Financial Administration (Public CAP. 5A
Enterprise Investment Fund) ss.8-10
8. (1) The initial expenditure incurred in establishing and admin-
Defrayment
istering the Fund shall be defrayed out of such moneys as Parliament of initial
expenses
may provide for the purpose.
and
temporary
(2) Any temporary insufficiency in the resources of the Fund to financing.
discharge the liabilities of the Fund shall be met from such sums as
Parliament may vote for the purpose by way of an advance.
(3) Any sum voted by Parliament pursuant to subsection (2) shall
be repaid out of the Fund as soon as practicable and paid into the
Consolidated Fund.
9. (1) The Accountant-General shall prepare quarterly reports of
Reports.
the accounts and economic activity of the Fund and shall deliver the
reports to the Minister responsible for Finance not later than 21 days
following the end of each quarter.
(2) The Minister responsible for Finance shall, as soon as
practicable after receiving a report referred to in subsection (1), cause
a copy to be laid on the table of both Houses.
10. The Minister responsible for Finance may, by order, amend
Amendment
the Schedule. of Schedule.
THE LAWS OF BARBADOS
Printed by the Government Printer, Bay Street, St. Michael
by the authority of the Government of Barbados
